Records.description: Three-loop sunrise-type vacuum diagram with four different masses in $D=4-2\varepsilon$ dimensions, computed in coordinate space via Bessel function integrals. Corresponds to $E(m_1^2,m_2^2,m_3^2,m_4^2)$ of Martin-Robertson and $M(1,1,1,1,0,0)$ of Freitas. Finite parts evaluated numerically using truncated Bessel integral identities.
Records.familyName: 3-loop sunrise vacuum (E) / 3-loop sunrise vacuum with dot (F)

Records.description: Three-loop four-propagator vacuum integral $U_3(m_1^2,m_2^2,m_3^2,m_4^2)$ with four independent internal masses and no external legs (basketball/banana-type topology: four edges between two vertices). Computed exactly in $D=4-2\varepsilon$ via Feynman parametrization, shown to be equivalent to a hypergeometric-theory derivation using modified Bessel function representations. Closed-form result given as triple-variable generalized hypergeometric (Lauricella $F_C^{(3)}$) series in mass ratios, plus associated systems of homogeneous linear PDEs to analytically continue beyond the convergent region via a finite-element method. Special cases worked out in Section 4: (4.1) $m_1=m_2$, (4.2) $m_1=0$, (4.3) $m_1=m_2=0$, expressed via Kampe de Feriet and Appell $F_4$ functions. Numerical cross-check against Martin 2017.

Three-loop sunrise-type vacuum diagram with one squared propagator (dot) and four different masses in D=4-2*eps dimensions. Corresponds to F(m1^2,m2^2,m3^2,m4^2) of Martin-Robertson and M(2,1,1,1,0,0) of Freitas. Computed in coordinate space via Bessel function techniques.

Four-propagator three-loop vacuum master integral with doubled first propagator. Symmetric under permutations of m2,m3,m4. Evaluated via dispersion integral. Note: in the original `1112|2||` representation, one propagator carries raised exponent (a=2).
